input: Create a periodic table explorer module. It should display the standard 18-column periodic table layout with all 118 elements. Each element cell shows atomic number, symbol, name, and atomic mass. Color-code elements by category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.). Clicking an element opens a detail panel with electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and a brief description. Include a search bar and category filter.
hint: Store all 118 elements as a static JSON file (data/elements.json) loaded at startup — do NOT hardcode in TypeScript. Layout the 18-column grid using CSS Grid with explicit row/column placement for lanthanides/actinides. Element data can be a static JSON array embedded in the module. Categories: alkali-metal, alkaline-earth, transition-metal, post-transition-metal, metalloid, nonmetal, halogen, noble-gas, lanthanide, actinide. Detail panel slides in from the right.

---
name: periodic-table
description: Interactive periodic table explorer with element details, search, and category filtering
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "3"
---

# Periodic Table Explorer

An interactive periodic table displaying all 118 elements in the standard 18-column layout. Each element cell shows its atomic number, symbol, name, and atomic mass, color-coded by category.

## Features

- **Full 118-element table** in standard layout with lanthanides and actinides in separate rows
- **Color-coded categories**: alkali metals, alkaline earth metals, transition metals, post-transition metals, metalloids, nonmetals, halogens, noble gases, lanthanides, actinides
- **Element detail panel**: click any element to view electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and a brief description
- **Search**: filter elements by name, symbol, or atomic number
- **Category filter**: show only elements of a specific category

## Usage

Navigate to the module to see the full periodic table. Use the search bar to find elements by name, symbol, or atomic number. Use the category dropdown to filter by element type. Click any element to open the detail panel.

- "atomicNumber": number (1-118)

- "oxidationStates": string (comma-separated like "+1, -1" or "N/A")
- "meltingPoint": number|null (in °C, null if unknown)
- "boilingPoint": number|null (in °C, null if unknown)
- "discoveryYear": number|null (null if known since antiquity)
- "description": string (1-2 sentence description of the element)
- "row": number (grid row position in 18-column periodic table)
- "col": number (grid column position in 18-column periodic table)

Grid positions for the standard 18-column layout:
- Row 1: H(col 1), He(col 18)
- Row 2: Li(1), Be(2), B(13), C(14), N(15), O(16), F(17), Ne(18)
- Row 3: Na(1), Mg(2), Al(13), Si(14), P(15), S(16), Cl(17), Ar(18)
- Row 4: K(1) through Kr(18) - all 18 columns filled
- Row 5: Rb(1) through Xe(18) - all 18 columns filled
- Row 6: Cs(1), Ba(2), Hf(4), Ta(5), W(6), Re(7), Os(8), Ir(9), Pt(10), Au(11), Hg(12), Tl(13), Pb(14), Bi(15), Po(16), At(17), Rn(18) — NO La here, it goes to row 9
- Row 7: Fr(1), Ra(2), Rf(4), Db(5), Sg(6), Bh(7), Hs(8), Mt(9), Ds(10), Rg(11), Cn(12), Nh(13), Fl(14), Mc(15), Lv(16), Ts(17), Og(18) — NO Ac here, it goes to row 10
- Row 9 (lanthanides): La(3), Ce(4), Pr(5), Nd(6), Pm(7), Sm(8), Eu(9), Gd(10), Tb(11), Dy(12), Ho(13), Er(14), Tm(15), Yb(16), Lu(17)
- Row 10 (actinides): Ac(3), Th(4), Pa(5), U(6), Np(7), Pu(8), Am(9), Cm(10), Bk(11), Cf(12), Es(13), Fm(14), Md(15), No(16), Lr(17)

Category assignments:
- Hydrogen is "nonmetal"
- Alkali metals: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr
- Alkaline earth metals: Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra
- Transition metals: Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Y, Zr, Nb, Mo, Tc, Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag, Cd, Hf, Ta, W, Re, Os, Ir, Pt, Au, Hg, Rf, Db, Sg, Bh, Hs
- Post-transition metals: Al, Ga, In, Sn, Tl, Pb, Bi, Nh, Fl, Mc, Lv
- Metalloids: B, Si, Ge, As, Sb, Te
- Nonmetals: H, C, N, O, P, S, Se
- Halogens: F, Cl, Br, I, At, Ts
- Noble gases: He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe, Rn, Og
- Lanthanides: La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Pm, Sm, Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, Yb, Lu
- Actinides: Ac, Th, Pa, U, Np, Pu, Am, Cm, Bk, Cf, Es, Fm, Md, No, Lr
- Unknown: Mt, Ds, Rg, Cn

The file should be a JSON array of 118 objects, sorted by atomicNumber. Make sure the data is scientifically accurate. Use the Write tool to write the file.

IMPORTANT: Ensure exactly 118 elements, each with all fields populated. Double-check the grid positions match the layout described above.
